folders.description:
# Main Package

This directory contains the main package for the application.

## Purpose
- Main application classes
- Business logic
- Service implementations
- Controllers
- Models

# Controllers

This directory contains REST controllers that handle HTTP requests.

## Purpose
- REST endpoints
- Request/response handling
- Input validation
- Response formatting

## Best Practices
- Use appropriate HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE)
- Implement proper error handling
- Use DTOs for request/response
- Follow REST naming conventions

# Services

This directory contains business logic and service implementations.

## Purpose
- Business logic implementation
- Data processing
- External service integration
- Transaction management

## Best Practices
- Keep services focused and single-responsibility
- Use interfaces for better testability
- Implement proper error handling
- Use appropriate transaction boundaries

# Repositories

This directory contains data access layer implementations.

## Purpose
- Database operations
- Entity management
- Query implementations
- Data persistence

## Best Practices
- Use Spring Data JPA repositories
- Implement custom queries when needed
- Use appropriate fetch strategies
- Handle database-specific operations

# Models

This directory contains domain models and entities.

## Purpose
- Entity classes
- DTOs (Data Transfer Objects)
- Value objects
- Domain models

## Best Practices
- Use JPA annotations appropriately
- Implement proper validation
- Use Lombok for reducing boilerplate
- Keep entities focused and clean

# Configuration

This directory contains application configuration classes.

## Purpose
- Bean configurations
- Security settings
- Database configuration
- External service configuration

## Best Practices
- Use configuration properties
- Implement proper security measures
- Use profiles for different environments
- Keep configurations modular

# Exceptions

This directory contains custom exceptions and error handling.

## Purpose
- Custom exception classes
- Global exception handlers
- Error response models
- Exception utilities

## Best Practices
- Create specific exception types
- Implement proper error messages
- Use appropriate HTTP status codes
- Log exceptions properly

# Utilities

This directory contains utility classes and helper methods.

## Purpose
- Helper methods
- Constants
- Common utilities
- Shared functionality

## Best Practices
- Keep utilities stateless
- Make methods pure when possible
- Document public methods
- Use appropriate access modifiers

# Resources

This directory contains application resources and configuration files.

## Purpose
- Application properties
- Static resources
- Templates
- Configuration files

## Best Practices
- Use YAML for complex configurations
- Separate properties by environment
- Keep sensitive data secure
- Use appropriate file formats

# Test Source Code

This directory contains test classes for the application.

## Purpose
- Unit tests
- Integration tests
- Test utilities
- Mock data

## Best Practices
- Follow testing pyramid
- Use appropriate testing frameworks
- Write meaningful test cases
- Maintain test data properly

# Test Resources

This directory contains resources specific to testing.

## Purpose
- Test properties
- Test data files
- Mock configurations
- Test utilities

## Best Practices
- Keep test data isolated
- Use appropriate test databases
- Maintain test configurations
- Follow testing conventions
